extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won stage 18 sprint after surviving two last-place finishes in Pyrenees, team FDJ controlled finale perfectly.
tactical_patterns: DÉMARE survived mountain stages in last place then recovered for sprint win—third-week recovery capacity underestimated in survivors.
FDJ paced too aggressively early but GUARNIERI delivered flawless final leadout, showing group sprint control over individual positioning.
ALAPHILIPPE peeled off 7km from finish to conserve energy for Stage 19 breakaway, pre-planning rest within transition stages.
THOMAS benefits from Stage 18 respite breaking Pyrenees into non-consecutive efforts, avoiding three-straight-days cracking risk.

rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Recovered from back-to-back lanterne rouge finishes to win sprint, exceptional third-week resilience for heavy sprinter.
rider_signals.GUARNIERI Jacopo: positive: Delivered textbook leadout after FDJ controlled stage aggressively from start, elite domestique execution under fatigue.
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: neutral: Had position but lacked final acceleration against pure sprinters, confirmed as tier-two fastman.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Sitting third overall but commentary questions his climbing form, expecting him sharper Stage 19 after one-off bad day.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Route design with transition stage suits his consistent-but-not-explosive style, avoiding consecutive mountain cracking risk.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Looked unusually exhausted despite safe sixth-place finish, third-week accumulation showing in facial expression.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Abandoned sprint 7km out to rest for Stage 19 breakaway hunt, mature energy management for multi-day objectives.
rider_signals.PHINNEY Taylor: positive: Ninth place mixing with specialist sprinters, dad former stage winner, criterium skills translating in third-week subdued racing.
